channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: SoFi Stadium workers vote to authorize strike ahead of World Cup
item.description: Negotiations between the union representing the workers, the hospitality group at the Los Angeles stadium and FIFA are set to continue Monday.
item.content:encoded:
Negotiations between the union representing the workers, the hospitality group at the Los Angeles stadium and FIFA are set to continue Monday.

(Image credit: Jae C. Hong)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Why one historian uses social media to remember D-Day in real time
item.description: At the National World War II Memorial, historian Alex Kershaw has found an unlikely way to keep D-Day alive: live social media posts timed to the events of June 6, 1944.
item.content:encoded: At the National World War II Memorial, historian Alex Kershaw has found an unlikely way to keep D-Day alive: live social media posts timed to the events of June 6, 1944.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Who will face Karen Bass? LA voters still waiting to find out
item.description: Results are still coming in from the mayoral primary in Los Angeles. LAist reporter Frank Stoltze discusses who may emerge to face Karen Bass in November.
item.content:encoded: Results are still coming in from the mayoral primary in Los Angeles. LAist reporter Frank Stoltze discusses who may emerge to face Karen Bass in November.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Israel has reportedly used white phosphorus near Lebanese cities and towns. What is it?
item.description: White phosphorus is not banned under international law, but can "create cruel injuries" and indiscriminate harm in civilian areas.
item.content:encoded:
White phosphorus is not banned under international law, but can "create cruel injuries" and indiscriminate harm in civilian areas.

(Image credit: Oliver Laban-Mattei)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Pope Leo says war with Iran is not a 'just war'
item.description: Pope Leo XIV said the war in Iran does not qualify as a "just war" according to Catholic teaching, while answering questions by journalists aboard the papal plane for his six-day visit to Spain.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV said the war in Iran does not qualify as a "just war" according to Catholic teaching, while answering questions by journalists aboard the papal plane for his six-day visit to Spain.

(Image credit: Alessandra Tarantino)
item.dc:creator: Claire Giangrave

item.title: Despite a competitive market, finding a summer job is highly beneficial for teens
item.description: Teenagers looking for summer jobs face a tough labor market. But the personal benefits are huge.
item.content:encoded:
Teenagers looking for summer jobs face a tough labor market. But the personal benefits are huge.

(Image credit: Etienne Laurent)
item.dc:creator: Dianna Douglas

item.title: As American elections become more tense, officials are turning to local police
item.description: Since the 2020 election, local law enforcement has increasingly been playing a bigger role in helping local officials secure elections.
item.content:encoded:
Since the 2020 election, local law enforcement has increasingly been playing a bigger role in helping local officials secure elections.

(Image credit: Ronda Churchill)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: A park famed for rare gorillas gears up to fight Ebola and protect its primates
item.description: In Virunga National Park, rangers are on the front lines — playing a critical role to contain the surging virus while coping with an upsurge in conflict-related violence.
item.content:encoded:
In Virunga National Park, rangers are on the front lines — playing a critical role to contain the surging virus while coping with an upsurge in conflict-related violence.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Israeli airstrikes kill 9 including Lebanese army officers after ceasefire deal
item.description: The Israeli military confirmed hitting a vehicle and said the incident is being reviewed. Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun called the strike "a flagrant violation to Lebanese sovereignty and international law."
item.content:encoded:
The Israeli military confirmed hitting a vehicle and said the incident is being reviewed. Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun called the strike "a flagrant violation to Lebanese sovereignty and international law."

(Image credit: Hussein Malla)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Deported Filipino sailors say they were falsely linked to child sexual abuse material
item.description: NPR has tracked deported Filipino sailors who say they were accused without evidence of possessing child sexual exploitation material. Almost none have been charged or prosecuted.
item.content:encoded: NPR has tracked deported Filipino sailors who say they were accused without evidence of possessing child sexual exploitation material. Almost none have been charged or prosecuted.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán
